title: Pre Instrumentality limbo recreation from information
display_title: Pre Instrumentality limbo recreation from information
wikitext:
[]: Pre-Instrumentality limbo: recreation from information?             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Apr 28, 2010 2:02 pm So, we all know how EoE went down: tanging, big epiphany, Yui/Rei/half the cast nags Shinji into changing his mind, "Kimochi warui." So, the A.T. fields are back, and anyone who wants to can come back. We don't know how many people came back besides Shinji and Asuka by the time of the final scene. Could be millions, could be none. So, it is probable that there is a sea of dissolved people out there, still in possesion of their recollections, identity and everything that defines them. Yet at the same time, they still float around in a big common primordial soup, sans body, existing as pure consciouness. At that point, it could be that though individuals know of themselves and others, they still merge in and out of each other, flickering between separation and togetherness. So what if in this rather peculiar state, inforamtion carried by conscious entities can take on a life of it's own? Memories, thoughts and perceptions of others and the self forming a soul? After all, what separates a soul from a batch of data? The A.T filed? But what makes one such thing manifset? Imagine, the memories about you carried by others creating duplicate or altered versions of your soul. In essence, do you think it is possible for souls to be created/recreated purly from data carried around in pre-Instrumentality limbo?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Apr 28, 2010 4:47 pm I don't think these souls actually have any ability to connect together. After all, Instrumentality was rejected, so they're separate souls in the same soup. They may not be aware of anything outside of their own thoughts, similar to many Shinji dreamtrips.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 29, 2010 3:31 am After 3I is over, the reinstated ATFs would ensure that there are borders between the various psyches once again (since that's what ATFs fundamentally are), so consciouses couldn't just drift in and out of each other like in HIP. The biggest challenges seem to be alluded to here: Kaworu: Reality is in an unfamiliar place, and dreams are within reality. Rei: And the truth is within your heart. Kaworu: For it is the hearts of people that create their appearance. Rei: And new images will change peoples' hearts and appearances. Images... the power of imagination... The power to create your own future, and to make time flow. Kaworu: But people must act of their own free will, or nothing will change at all. Rei: So you must find your lost self on your own... Even if you lose your own words, or become lost in others' words.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Apr 29, 2010 1:39 pm Reichu wrote: How can you feel anything BUT lost?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat May 01, 2010 12:25 am Reichu wrote: Well, from where I'm standing, taken at face value it all reads like a fairly incomprehensible train of thought and I still don't if there is any real logic behind the jumps from one point to another... (Maybe it's really obvious and my brain just isn't working tonight.) But that aside, the stuff about "acting of your own free will" and "finding your lost self" seem to resound somewhat with what we're talking about.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat May 01, 2010 12:39 am For what it's worth: Rei's final 2 lines (losing yourself in others' words, etc) seem referenced in the final scene, in a way. Shinji sobbing instead of speaking after the strangle attempt, Asuka's words contrasting with her actions, the caress. /Making sense of Rei's Rei-ness. That aside, I agree with what everybody else said in terms of the post-3I souls business.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Tue May 04, 2010 6:34 pm I like to think of the soul as a recipe to everything that makes you, you. Your memories, DNA sequencing, what you like and what you don't, every cut and every bruise you've ever had. The post-3I soup is the ingredients. Every cell, acid or element that makes up your body. If you have the recipe and the ingredients all you need is the will. So to answer your question, yes. If you had everything you needed it would be possible to recreate a soul.
